description: Milestone 9 — Items (passive accessories, Ragnarok-themed). 6 items mirroring the M8 weapon pattern: ItemDef table with L1-L5 multiplicative effect curves, mixed level-up choice pool drawing from weapons AND items with equal weighting, single getItemMultiplier(player, effect) helper for all effect application (rule 12: dispatches on effect enum only, never name). Items: Ifrit's Talisman (damage_mult), Wind of Verdure (cooldown_mult), Apple of Idun (max_hp_mult), Sleipnir (speed_mult), Magnifier (magnet_mult), Bunny Top Hat (xp_mult). Touch points: 5+ damage emit sites, 5 weapon cooldown set sites, tickPlayers movement step, tickGems magnet radius + xp gain, resolveLevelUp for max HP apply. Two manual BLOCKING gates: mid-milestone playtest (US-006), final playtest (US-008). Source PRD: tasks/prd-m9-items.md. CLAUDE.md rules binding — schema in shared/schema.ts (Player.items via declare+defineTypes per landmine #1), messages in shared/messages.ts (LevelUpOfferedEvent.choices shape change from number[] to {type, index}[]), logic in shared/rules.ts, no Math.random in gameplay code, NO name-based branching for any item, tick order from rule 11 unchanged (no new tick functions in M9).

userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Architecture review checkpoint
userStories.description: As the project owner (Luke), I need to review the ItemDef shape, the full ITEM_KINDS table with L1-L5 value curves, the schema additions, the LevelUpOfferedEvent shape change, and the touch-points list BEFORE any implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Design doc / PR-stub presents ItemDef shape { name, effect: ItemEffect, values: number[] }
Design doc presents ItemEffect enum 'damage_mult' | 'cooldown_mult' | 'max_hp_mult' | 'speed_mult' | 'magnet_mult' | 'xp_mult'
Design doc presents the full 6-item ITEM_KINDS table with L1-L5 values arrays per the PRD §3
Design doc lists schema additions: ItemState class (kind:uint8, level:uint8), Player.items:ArraySchema<ItemState> — declare-style + defineTypes per landmine #1
Design doc shows LevelUpOfferedEvent.choices shape change from number[] to Array<{type:'weapon'|'item', index:number}>
Design doc clarifies LevelUpResolvedEvent shape (whether to add itemKind field or rename to picked:{type, index})
Design doc lists getItemMultiplier(player, effect):number helper signature and the call sites (5 damage emit + 5 weapon cooldown set + tickPlayers + tickGems + resolveLevelUp)
Open-question resolutions: L5 cap behavior (no-op vs re-roll), HP heal on Apple of Idun (full diff vs half), item icons (emoji vs shape vs text)
Luke has explicitly approved OR provided concrete changes that have been incorporated
No work on US-002+ begins until this story is closed
userStories.notes: Approved 2026-05-10 by Luke. Architecture decisions captured at the PRD level (tasks/prd-m9-items.md): mixed weapons+items level-up pool, 6 items with L1-L5 multiplicative curves, RO-themed names, getItemMultiplier helper as single dispatch point (rule 12). Four open-question resolutions: A1 L5 cap = silent no-op (re-roll deferred), A2 LevelUpResolvedEvent rename to picked:{type, index}, A3 Apple of Idun heals full diff at pickup, A4 emoji placeholders for item icons in US-007 polish.

userStories.title: Item infrastructure — schema, table, helper, mixed level-up choice pool
userStories.description: As the simulation, I need the data model and dispatch helper for items to exist before any individual item's effect is wired in. Produces a complete but inert item layer.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/shared/src/items.ts exports ItemEffect enum, ItemDef type, ITEM_KINDS:readonly ItemDef[] (with all 6 entries — effects not yet wired in this US), itemValueAt(def, level), isItemEffect type guard
packages/shared/src/schema.ts: new ItemState class with kind:uint8, level:uint8, declare-style + defineTypes (landmine #1); Player.items:ArraySchema<ItemState> added (declare + ctor + defineTypes)
packages/shared/src/rules.ts: new exported getItemMultiplier(player, effect):number walks player.items, multiplicatively accumulates values where def.effect === effect, returns 1 if no matching items
LevelUpOfferedEvent.choices shape changed in shared/messages.ts from number[] to Array<{type:'weapon'|'item', index:number}>
resolveLevelUp draws from WEAPON_KINDS.length + ITEM_KINDS.length mixed pool with equal weighting via state.rng() (rule 6); encodes each as {type, index}
resolveLevelUp item pickup path: if player has the item, increments item.level (capped at 5); else pushes new ItemState with kind=index, level=1
Weapon path unchanged in observable behavior (non-regression)
LevelUpResolvedEvent updated per US-001 decision (either add itemKind field with -1 sentinel OR rename to picked:{type, index})
Vitest: existing M7 level-up tests pass unchanged for weapon-only choices
Vitest: rolling choices from mixed pool produces both weapon and item entries deterministically given a fixed seed
Vitest: applying an item choice creates a new ItemState at level 1; second pickup increments; capped at 5
Vitest: getItemMultiplier with no items returns 1, with one item at L3 returns the L3 value, with two same-effect items multiplies them
Schema encoder integration test still passes (landmine #1 regression guard)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. shared/src/items.ts: NEW file — ItemEffect enum (damage_mult/cooldown_mult/max_hp_mult/speed_mult/magnet_mult/xp_mult), ItemDef shape, ITEM_KINDS table with all 6 entries (Ifrit's Talisman, Wind of Verdure, Apple of Idun, Sleipnir, Magnifier, Bunny Top Hat), itemValueAt clamp helper (mirrors statsAt from weapons.ts), isItemEffect type guard, NEUTRAL_MULTIPLIER=1.0 constant. schema.ts: NEW ItemState class (kind:uint8, level:uint8, declare-style + defineTypes per landmine #1); NEW LevelUpChoice class with type:uint8 + index:uint8 fields; LEVEL_UP_CHOICE_WEAPON=0 and LEVEL_UP_CHOICE_ITEM=1 sentinel constants exported; Player.items:ArraySchema<ItemState> added (declare + ctor + defineTypes); Player.levelUpChoices type changed from ArraySchema<number> to ArraySchema<LevelUpChoice> (breaking change to schema layout — encoder regression test in server integration verifies). messages.ts: LevelUpChoicePayload type ({type:'weapon'|'item', index:number}); LevelUpOfferedEvent.choices is now LevelUpChoicePayload[] (was number[]); LevelUpResolvedEvent renamed weaponKind+newWeaponLevel → picked:LevelUpChoicePayload + newLevel (applies to either weapon or item picks). rules.ts: NEW exported getItemMultiplier(player, effect):number helper — single dispatch point for ALL effect lookup (rule 12 — dispatches on effect enum only, never name); resolveLevelUp signature change from (player, weaponKind, emit, autoPicked) to (player, picked:{type, index}, emit, autoPicked) — branches internally on picked.type for weapon vs item paths; item path increments existing item.level or pushes new ItemState at L1, capped at ITEM_KINDS[i].values.length (L5 cap silent no-op per A1); max_hp_mult item pickup immediately recomputes player.maxHp = floor(PLAYER_MAX_HP * getItemMultiplier(p, 'max_hp_mult')) AND heals the diff per A3; tickXp mixed pool roll: draws from poolSize = WEAPON_KINDS.length + ITEM_KINDS.length (14 entries), encodes each as LevelUpChoice with type 0/1; tickLevelUpDeadlines updated to decode LevelUpChoice and pass {type, index} to resolveLevelUp. GameRoom: imports LEVEL_UP_CHOICE_ITEM constant; level_up_choice message handler decodes the schema choice's type into the string literal before calling resolveLevelUp. shared/index.ts exports items.js. Client: LevelUpOverlay describeChoice() rewritten with weapon vs item branch (UI polish in US-007), reads ITEM_KINDS for item names + L→L+1 preview; LevelUpFlashVfx unchanged (only reads playerId). Test updates: 4 existing resolveLevelUp/tickXp/tickLevelUpDeadlines tests reworked for new API (push LevelUpChoice schema entries via pushChoice helper, pass {type, index} to resolveLevelUp, assert resolved.picked + resolved.newLevel); levelUpReconnect integration test reworked to compare {type, index} structurally instead of reference equality. 14 new tests: getItemMultiplier × 5 (default 1.0, single item L3, multi-item stack, level=0 ignored, all 6 effects neutral); resolveLevelUp item path × 6 (first pickup level 1, second pickup increments, L5 cap silent no-op, Apple of Idun heals diff at half HP, full HP scaling, low HP capping); tickXp mixed pool × 2 (both weapon and item entries roll across 100 samples, identical seed produces identical choices); Player.items default × 1. pnpm typecheck + 266 shared (was 252) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass — server integration encoder regression confirms ItemState + LevelUpChoice schemas + Player.items + Player.levelUpChoices new shape ride Colyseus correctly (landmine #1 avoided).

userStories.title: Damage Up + Cooldown Reduction items (Ifrit's Talisman + Wind of Verdure)
userStories.description: As a player, I want items that scale my weapon damage and reduce cooldowns so the build layer meaningfully strengthens whatever weapons I picked.
acceptanceCriteria: ITEM_KINDS[0] is Ifrit's Talisman with effect 'damage_mult', values [1.10, 1.20, 1.30, 1.40, 1.50]
ITEM_KINDS[1] is Wind of Verdure with effect 'cooldown_mult', values [0.95, 0.90, 0.85, 0.80, 0.75]

All damage emit sites in rules.ts multiply damage by getItemMultiplier(player, 'damage_mult'): tickWeapons orbit arm, runMeleeArcSwing (AFTER crit multiplier so crit and item stack multiplicatively), runAuraTick, tickProjectiles, tickBoomerangs
tickBoomerangs BAKES damage_mult into BloodPool.damagePerTick at spawn (NOT at DoT time) so a mid-flight item pickup doesn't retroactively change in-flight pools — single-writer-at-spawn pattern
tickWeapons cooldown set sites multiply stats.cooldown by getItemMultiplier(player, 'cooldown_mult') in projectile/melee_arc/boomerang/aura arms
Aura arm cooldown_mult applies to tickIntervalMs/1000 so Kronos ticks faster with cooldown reduction
NO name-based branching for either item (rule 12 grep verified)
Vitest: Ifrit's Talisman L3 (1.30×) on Damascus crit-hit deals damage × crit_mult × 1.30 (multiplicative stacking)
Vitest: Wind of Verdure L2 (0.90×) sets Bolt cooldown to base × 0.90 after fire
Vitest: two same-effect items multiply (defensive — only one of each kind in practice)
Vitest: Bloody Axe blood pool spawned with Ifrit's L3 has damagePerTick baked at base × 1.30; subsequent Ifrit's L5 pickup does NOT retroactively change in-flight pools
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. damage_mult wired into ALL 5 weapon families: tickWeapons projectile arm BAKES proj.damage = stats.damage * getItemMultiplier(player, 'damage_mult') at fire (single-writer-at-spawn — mid-flight pickup doesn't retro-buff in-flight projectiles); orbit arm applies at emit time (orbit is continuous, no spawn event) — computed once per orb cycle for performance; runMeleeArcSwing applies at emit AFTER crit multiplier (final = base × critMult × itemMult, multiplicative stacking); runAuraTick applies at tick time (no spawn event); runBoomerangThrow BAKES both boomerang.damage AND boomerang.bloodPoolDamagePerTick at throw — fractional pool damage allowed on the JS struct, floored when assigned to BloodPool.damagePerTick uint16 schema field at the pool-spawn site in tickBoomerangs. cooldown_mult wired into 4 weapon arms (projectile/melee_arc/boomerang/aura) — applied at cooldownRemaining-reset sites; orbit DELIBERATELY excluded (continuous behavior, no discrete cooldown — orbit already scales via orbAngularSpeed + orbCount per-level). Aura's cooldownRemaining (repurposed as 'seconds until next aura tick' per US-010) ALSO scales with cooldown_mult so Kronos ticks faster with Wind's. Tests (10 new): Ifrit's × 6 — Bolt projectile L3 (1.30×) baked at fire = 13 damage, Damascus crit + Ifrit's L5 stacks (10 × 2 crit × 1.50 = 30), Kronos aura tick L2 (1.20×) at emit = 12, Bloody Axe + Ifrit's L4 (1.40×) bakes both axe damage (42) AND pool damage (11.2 fractional, floored to 11 in schema), single-writer-at-spawn (mid-flight Ifrit's pickup doesn't retro-buff in-flight projectile damage — proj.damage stays at fire-time value), orbit + Ifrit's L1 (1.10×) at emit = 6.6; Wind's × 4 — Bolt L2 (0.90×) cooldown 0.60→0.54, Damascus L5 (0.75×) cooldown 0.35→0.2625, Kronos aura tick L3 (0.85×) interval 500ms→425ms, stacking Ifrit's + Wind's both apply independently (damage × cooldown). NO name-based branching anywhere (rule 12 verified). pnpm typecheck + 276 shared (was 266) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass — server integration encoder regression confirms BloodPool.damagePerTick still rides Colyseus correctly after the multiplier change (Math.floor at assignment keeps uint16 valid).

userStories.title: Max HP + Movement Speed items (Apple of Idun + Sleipnir)
userStories.description: As a player, I want items that increase survivability and mobility so the build layer affects both offense AND defense.
acceptanceCriteria: ITEM_KINDS[2] is Apple of Idun with effect 'max_hp_mult', values [1.20, 1.40, 1.60, 1.80, 2.00]
ITEM_KINDS[3] is Sleipnir with effect 'speed_mult', values [1.05, 1.10, 1.15, 1.20, 1.25] (small scaling to avoid feel-breaking sprint at L5)

resolveLevelUp Apple of Idun pickup: recomputes player.maxHp = Math.floor(PLAYER_MAX_HP * getItemMultiplier(player, 'max_hp_mult')) AND heals the player for the diff (player.hp += diff, capped at new maxHp)
tickPlayers movement step multiplies PLAYER_SPEED by getItemMultiplier(player, 'speed_mult')
Vitest: full-HP player picking Apple of Idun L1 sees maxHp scale to floor(PLAYER_MAX_HP*1.20) and hp matches (full health preserved)
Vitest: half-HP player picking Apple of Idun L1 sees maxHp scale and hp increase by the diff (still not full health, but ratio preserved)
Vitest: player with Sleipnir L3 (1.15×) moves at PLAYER_SPEED * 1.15 * dt per tick on a unit-length inputDir
Existing tickPlayers tests pass unchanged for players with no speed_mult items (non-regression)
NO name-based branching (rule 12 grep clean)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. Apple of Idun (max_hp_mult) — max_hp recompute + heal-diff at pickup was wired in US-002's resolveLevelUp implementation; the US-002 test suite (full HP/half HP/low HP pickup) already covers the AC. Sleipnir (speed_mult) — added const speedMult = getItemMultiplier(p, 'speed_mult') in tickPlayers, multiplied into p.x/z step. Default 1.0 (no items) preserves M5-M8 behavior — existing tickPlayers tests stay green (non-regression). Tests (4 new for Sleipnir): L3 1.15× expected speed * 1.15 * dt, L5 1.25× max-boost, no-item baseline (PLAYER_SPEED * dt unchanged), downed players still skip movement even with Sleipnir (existing tickPlayers gate). NO name-based branching (rule 12 clean). pnpm typecheck + 280 shared (was 276) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass.

userStories.title: Magnet Range + XP Gain items (Magnifier + Bunny Top Hat)
userStories.description: As a player, I want items that help me collect more gems faster and gain more XP from each gem — the build layer affects the loop of gem → XP → level-up.
acceptanceCriteria: ITEM_KINDS[4] is Magnifier with effect 'magnet_mult', values [1.25, 1.50, 1.75, 2.00, 2.25] (steeper scaling — magnet feel benefits)
ITEM_KINDS[5] is Bunny Top Hat with effect 'xp_mult', values [1.10, 1.20, 1.30, 1.40, 1.50]

tickGems pickup check uses per-player effective radius: GEM_PICKUP_RADIUS * getItemMultiplier(player, 'magnet_mult'); multiplier computed ONCE per player at the start of tickGems for performance (not per gem)
tickGems XP gain on pickup: xpGained = Math.floor(gem.value * getItemMultiplier(player, 'xp_mult')); applied to both player.xp AND player.xpGained cumulative counter
Vitest: player with Magnifier L4 (2.00×) picks up gem at exactly GEM_PICKUP_RADIUS*2.00-epsilon (would have been out of range without item)
Vitest: player with Bunny Top Hat L2 (1.20×) picking up a gem.value=10 gains floor(10*1.20)=12 XP
Vitest: player with Bunny Top Hat L5 (1.50×) picking up a gem.value=1 gains floor(1*1.50)=1 XP (boundary — single-value gems don't show the multiplier until ≥2 value)
Existing tickGems tests pass unchanged for players with no multipliers (non-regression)
NO name-based branching (rule 12 grep clean)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. tickGems refactored to precompute per-player effective radius² AND xp multiplier ONCE at the start of the tick (NOT per gem) — performance: O(gems × players × constant) instead of O(gems × players × items). playerRadiusSq Map and playerXpMult Map populated by walking state.players once via getItemMultiplier(p, 'magnet_mult') / 'xp_mult'. fallbackRadiusSq = GEM_PICKUP_RADIUS² for defensive case (player not in cache — should never happen since seed loop covers all). Per-gem inner loop reads from Map by sessionId. xp gain: const xpMult = playerXpMult.get(collector.sessionId) ?? 1; xpGain = floor(gem.value * xpMult); applied to both player.xp AND player.xpGained cumulative counter. Event 'value' field stays the RAW gem.value (it's a gem property; the player's actual gain may differ — documented inline). Tests (7 new): Magnifier × 2 (L4 2.00× picks up gem at 1.99×GEM_PICKUP_RADIUS, no-Magnifier doesn't pick up at 1.5×radius); Bunny Top Hat × 4 (L2 1.20× on value=10 gives 12 xp, L5 1.50× on value=1 gives 1 xp boundary, event.value stays raw gem.value while internal xp DID multiply, no-Bunny gives exact 10 xp non-regression); Magnifier+Bunny stack × 1 (both effects independent — gem at 2×radius picked up AND xp = floor(10 * 1.50) = 15). NO name-based branching (rule 12 clean). pnpm typecheck + 287 shared (was 280) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass.

userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Mid-milestone playtest checkpoint
userStories.description: As the project owner (Luke), I need to play with all 6 items live before committing to UI polish work. Items fundamentally change the level-up decision space.
acceptanceCriteria: Two clients can join, level up, and see weapons AND items appear in choice pools across multiple runs
Each of the 6 items, picked: visibly works (damage numbers, cooldowns, movement, magnet radius, XP gain)
No regressions: M5/M6/M7/M8 behavior (Bolt, Orbit, the 6 M8 weapons, status effects, blood pools) all still works
grep returns NOTHING for `name === "(Ifrit's Talisman|Wind of Verdure|Apple of Idun|Sleipnir|Magnifier|Bunny Top Hat)"` across packages/{shared,server,client}/src (CLAUDE.md rule 12 verification)
Two-client determinism: damage numbers, projectile paths, item effects match between two clients
Luke has explicitly approved feel OR filed concrete tuning changes that have been applied and re-tested
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
No work on US-007+ begins until this story is closed

userStories.title: Level-up UI polish — item vs weapon distinction
userStories.description: As a player, when the level-up panel appears, I should immediately see which of the 3 choices are weapons and which are items.
acceptanceCriteria: LevelUpOverlay.tsx reads the choice payload's `type` field and renders item choices visually distinct (item border or background tint — pick gold; or label 'ITEM' on item cards)
Item description text reads effect + current level value: e.g., 'Ifrit's Talisman L1 → +10% weapon damage'
If player already has the item, choice card shows upgrade preview: e.g., 'L2 (currently L1) → +20% weapon damage'
Item icons: emoji placeholders or simple shapes are acceptable (no asset hunt this milestone)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Final playtest checkpoint
userStories.description: As the project owner (Luke), I want a real session with friends before declaring M9 complete. Items significantly change build space; verify the loop is fun, not just functional.
acceptanceCriteria: Real multi-client session played end-to-end
All 8 weapons + 6 items confirmed to (a) appear in choice pools, (b) work as expected, (c) have visually readable presentation in the level-up overlay
No 'obviously broken' interactions (Apple of Idun heal exploits, infinite XP loops, item stacks that crash)
Performance: 200 enemies + 2 players each running 4-5 weapons + 3-4 items hold 60fps client / 20Hz server
Balance feedback captured for a follow-up tuning milestone (NOT addressed this milestone)
Final commit on main closes US-008 and the milestone
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
